summary: Applied the Grafana runtime hardening on monitoring-lv3, verified the public access controls, and restored Prometheus SLO rule loading with the corrected latency-rule generator output.

verification.observed: During `make live-apply-service service=grafana env=production`, the monitoring role passed the ADR 0140 checks that verify dashboard redirects, `https://grafana.lv3.org/api/health` returning `404`, and login-header stripping at the public edge.
`curl -s http://127.0.0.1:9090/api/v1/rules` on `monitoring-lv3` returned rule groups including both `slo_recording_rules` and `slo_alert_rules` after the corrected `slo_rules.yml` was applied.

The trailing shared Alertmanager validation in the same converge path still expects the controller-local file `.local/ntfy/alertmanager-password.txt`; its absence in this worktree did not roll back the Grafana or Prometheus changes already applied.

notes: Before the SLO generator fix, `promtool check config /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml` on `monitoring-lv3` rejected the generated `keycloak-latency` rules because the latency recording expressions used an invalid range selector on a comparison expression.
After applying the corrected recording rules from source commit `8e36e3f`, Prometheus loaded the shared SLO rule groups successfully and the Grafana-facing ADR 0140 verification contract passed.
